title: Mobile + desktop parity — every demo usable on both, or honestly unsupported with recorded evidence
author: cross-project-invariant
policy: CLAUDE.md → 'Mobile + desktop parity' (canonical policy, embedded verbatim)

assertions.description: Every published demo has a support record for BOTH mobile (≈360×740, DPR3, touch) and desktop (≈1280×800, mouse+keyboard): each class is ok, or unsupported WITH evidence — never left untested/broken for a touched demo.
On each class, document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth + 1: no unintended horizontal overflow of the page.
On each class, no interactive control is positioned off-viewport or clipped so it cannot be seen or operated.
On mobile, primary interactive controls meet an ≈44px minimum tap-target size.
On each class, focus order is logical and focus is visibly indicated for keyboard operation.
On each class, dialogs/popovers/menus open, position on-screen, dismiss, and trap focus correctly.
Where relevant, the demo uses dynamic viewport units (dvh/svh, not 100vh traps) and respects safe-area insets, verified on mobile.
On each class, loading / progress / error / retry states are present and usable for any async work the demo performs.
On each class, initial load and the primary interaction produce zero uncaught console errors (an expected unsupported/flag fallback logged intentionally is not an error).
On each class, no same-origin demo request fails (4xx/5xx/blocked) during load and the primary interaction.

When a platform feature/model/runtime is genuinely unavailable on a class, the demo degrades honestly (labelled needs-WebGPU / needs-more-memory / too-large-for-this-device with requirements + a fallback), never a blank panel, a faked result, or a control hidden/disabled without explanation. A genuinely-unavailable class is recorded unsupported WITH evidence, never a fake pass; device-unavailable during a run is blocked, never a pass.
